titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the John B. Lindner Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Jeffrey Coutts and Martha Lund Smalley

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday August 18, 2020 at 22:25

did.abstract: This collection documents selected activities of John B. Lindner, including his work with the National Council of Churches US/USSR Church Relations Committee, the "By Faith" project to prepare a video and book on the history of the ecumenical Christian student movement in the US, the Frontier Internship in Mission program, and projects related to Middle East policy and ecumenical formation.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John B. Lindner Papers (RG 208), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John B. Lindner received a B.S. degree from Youngstown State University in 1970 and an M.Div from McCormick Theological Seminary in 1975. A minister in the Presbyterian Church, U.S.A., he spent over a quarter century providing leadership for international, ecumenical/interfaith programs of the PCUSA, the National Council of Churches (NCCCUSA) and the World Council of Churches (WCC). He served as writer for the NCCCUSA's Middle East Policy and directed its program of US-USSR Church Relations. He served the WCC as director of planning and development for the Ecumenical Institute at Bossey. Lindner served as Director of the Department for External Relations and Associate Dean for External Relations at Yale Divinity School from 2002 to 2013. Lindner is co-author of "By Faith: Christian Students Among the Cloud of Witnesses" (1991, Friendship Press) a history of the ecumenical Christian student movement in the US and co-issue editor of a special issue of the journal Theological Education (vol. 34, autumn 1997) on "Ecumenical Formation: A Methodology for a Pluralistic Age. The Case of the Ecumenical Institute at Bossey".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection documents selected activities of John B. Lindner, focusing on his ecumenical and interfaith work during the 1980s and 1990s. It provides a valuable record of the "on-the-ground" operations of organizations and programs that sought to advance and document ecumenical work.

Series I documents Lindner's work on the National Council of Churches US/USSR Church Relations Committee from 1983 to 1988. Memos, reports, and audio-visual materials document the work of the committee and the trips to Russia and visits from Russian church figures that it sponsored. Background material provides an overview of US/USSR church relations.

Series II focuses on the preparation of a video and book on the history of the ecumenical student Christian movement in America. The video was prepared for a national student gathering sponsored by the Council for Ecumenical Student Christian Ministry in 1990. The book,"By Faith: Chrsitian Students among the Cloud of Witnesses" (1991), was edited by John B. Lindner, Alva I. Cox, Jr., and Linda-Marie Delloff. Numerous former leaders and participants in ecumenical student Christian movements were contacted to provide reminiscences, essays, and consultation in preparation of "By Faith." See also the Alva I. Cox, Jr. Papers (YDSL Record Group No. 179).

Series III documents Lindner's work on the International Coordinating Committee of the Frontier Internship program. This material provides a valuable complement to documentation of the Frontier Internship in Mission program found in the Margaret Flory Papers (YDSL Record Group No. 86).

Series IV relates to Lindner's work in preparing the 1980 Middle East Policy Statement of the National Council of Churches in the U.S.A. while Series V documents Lindner's work in the area of ecumenical formation.
Series VI contains writings, sermons, and talks by Lindner. Additional writings and talks can be found amongst the topically arranged documentation in Series I - V.

list.item: I. National Council of Churches US/USSR Church Relations Committee
III. Frontier Internship in Mission Program
IV. National Council of Churches Middle East Policy

subject.part: College students -- Religious life -- United States -- Societies, etc. -- History

did.unittitle: National Council of Churches US/USSR Church Relations Committee
Memos, minutes related to formation of committee
Other Committees: Children's Committee, Division of Overseas Ministries, U.S. U.S.S.R. Fit Committee
Committee Collected Material: Report, Notes, Article, Newsletter
1983 Peacemaking Seminar to Russia: Schedule, Journal, Postcard
1984 NCC Visit to Russia : Memos, Guidelines, Applications
1984 Soviet Ecumenical Visit: Itinerary, List of members in delegation
1985 Trip to Geneva Planning: Memos, Proposals, Program
1985 Trip to Geneva: Memos, Programs from event, Statement of church leaders, Copy of sermon
1985 Trip to Russia: Memos related to budget, Schedules
1987 U.S. Soviet Summit Washington D.C.: Brochures, Prayer Pamphlets
1987 Trip to Russia: Schedules, Participant Information
1988 Trip to Russia: Memos, Participant Information
1988 Trip to Russia: Participant Information, Memos, Questionnaires, Schedules
1988 Russia Trip: Participant Information, Briefing Manual, Schedules
Russia Trips Background Material 1984-1987: Budget Information, History of U.S. U.S.S.R. Church Relations
Russia Trips Background Materials 1992: Participant Information
Russia Trips Background Materials No Date: Song Books, Participants Directories, Russian Language Primer
Russia Trips Background Information: Participant Information, participant manual
John Lindner Russia Notes: Personnel Information, Scheduling
John Lindner Russia Notes: Participant Information, Scheduling
Friendship Press Collected Material: Maps of Russia, USSR Christianity Magazine
Friendship Press Collected Materials: Children's Book: Christianity in USSR, Guide to Book
Miscellaneous Collected Material: Church Pamphlets, Pictures of Russia
Pictures of Russia Trips: Contact Negatives
Pictures of Russia Trips: Contact Negatives
Pictures of Russia Trips: Contact Negatives
Pictures of Russia Trips: Developed Pictures,Slides 1984-1985
Discovering Our Oneness: Audio Cassette Tape and Photo Negatives
Sights and Sounds of the Churches of the U.S.S.R.: Audio Cassette Tape and Photo Negatives
Committee records; sermons by Lindner related to USSR visit; project proposal; CPC Consultation on Disarmament (Budapest)
Trip briefing speech by Lindner; notes from Friendship Press Board speech; Program Coordinator's Report; reports on US/USSR program
Committee records; report; pamphlet: "An Ecumenical Experiment in Human Rights" by Theo Tschuy
Letter of resignation; pamphlet: "One Thousand Years: Stories from the History of Christianity in the USSR 988-1988" by J. Martin Bailey

"Perestroika: some preliminary comments" by Ninan Koshy (WCC Commission of the Churches on International Affairs); typescript report on relationships between NCC and church of the Soviet Union 1956-1988
Report of NCC delegation to USSR; Coordinating Committee recommendations; article by Lindner: "The Reemergence of the National Council of Churches"
Documents from the files of Barbara Green and Alan Geyer, including regarding "Choose Life" gatherings, 1980 Soviet invasion of Afghanistan, US-Soviet relations
MIRror newsletter of NCC Europe office; Informational Bulletin, Department for External Church Relations, Moscow Patriarchate
Correspondence with Lilly Endowment, potential project participants, draft outlines
Correspondence, consultation at Stony Point Center
Regarding use and study guides, letters of thanks to contributors,
Early drafts for introduction and chapter 1 of "By Faith: Christian Students among the Cloud of Witness"
Video: "By Faith : Christian Students Among the Cloud of Witnesses" with study guide (VHS and DVD copies)
Publicity materials and review by John Boonstra
By Robert Bilheimer, Adele Cloutier, John Deschner, Dan Dodson
By Paul Hilmer, Jean Lesher, Edward Nestingen, William Roy, Richard Schaull, Steve Schomberg, Samuel Slie, Franklin Woo
"Farewell UCM" by Leon Howell in "Humanity"; "The Student Christian Movement" in "Liberty and Justice for All: Racial Reform and the Social Gospel"
Council for Ecumenical Student Christian Ministry, including program for "Celebrate" national ecumenical gathering of student Christian conference
Typescript, including "Timeline of U.S. Campus Ministries History, 1706-1987"; "Alumni of the SCM in the United States"
Miscellaneous handwritten notes by Lindner re. "In Faith" project
International Coordinating Committee meeting in Dakar, Senegal
"Blueprints": A Journal of Frontier Interns in Mission (two issues)
"The Frontier Internship Program: A Ten-Year Evaluation, 1961-1971", including "An Overview After Ten Years" by Margaret Flory
Reports related to internationalization of Frontier Internship program
Transcript of telephone conversation with Margaret Flory
Report regarding Middle East Frontier Intern project; memo
National Council of Churches Middle East policy
Regarding preparation of Middle East policy statement
Policy Statement on the Middle East and Study Document
Middle East Council of Churches inaugural assemby
Research internship with Middle East Council of Churches
Church in the Middle East background material
Contents of special issue of Theological Education on ecumenical formation edited by Lindner
"Bossey's Ecumenical Formation: A Methodology for a Pluralistic Age" by Heidi Hadsell and John Lindner; architectural renderings for Bossey addition
McCormick Theological Seminary research project: "A Decade of Travel Seminars: A Retrospective Inventory" by John Lindner and Eileen Lindner
Documents related to the National Council of Churches of Christ in the U.S.A. compiled by Lindner for discussion by Presbyterian Church (USA)
Paper for NCC Staff Conference: "Conciliar and Coalitional Ecumenism"; "Remarks to Presbyterian Staff Coordinating Cabinet" by John Lindner, Ecumenical Officer for the PCUSA to the NCC (1987-1992)
Photo publication from World Council of Churches Seventh Assembly, Canberra

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains a selection of materials written by Lindner. Additional papers and articles by Lindner are available in the topical groupings of Series I - V.

did.unittitle: Writings: untitled paper related to University Christian Movement, which folded in 1969; poem; "From Mainline to Minority: 'Moderate' and 'Liberal' Protestant Churches in America"
Plaque presented to Lindner by YDS Racial Ethnic Reunion
